company: GE Medical Systems Ultrasound and Primary Care Diagnostics
intended_use: The Venue Go is a general purpose diagnostic ultrasound system for use by qualified and trained healthcare professionals for ultrasound imaging, measurement, display and analysis of the human body and
intended_use_full: The Venue Go is a general purpose diagnostic ultrasound system for use by qualified and trained healthcare professionals for ultrasound imaging, measurement, display and analysis of the human body and fluid. Venue Go is intended to be used in a hospital or medical clinic. Venue Go clinical applications include: abdominal (GYN and Urology), thoracic/pleural, ophthalmic, Fetal/OB, Small Organ (including breast, testes, thyroid), Vascular/Peripheral vascular, neonatal and adult cephalic, pediatric, musculoskeletal (conventional and superficial), cardiac (adults and pediatric), Transrectal, Transvaginal, Transesophageal, Intraoperative (vascular) and interventional guidance (includes tissue biopsy, fluid drainage, vascular and non-vascular access).
target_condition: Nerve block procedures requiring nerve detection during scouting
ground_truth: Ground truth annotations obtained from a single clinical expert marking anatomical area of nerves in individual frames from scouting sequences.
other_metrics: Sequence accuracy requirement: At least 70% of sequences meaningfully detected, and at least 80% of meaningfully detected sequences successfully detected (meeting frame accuracy criteria). Performance verified via frame accuracy on all demographic subgroups (Gender M/F, Age <60 / >=60, BMI <=25 / >25) and all supported nerve block locations and probe types.
